name: Asian Counseling and Referral Service
notes: For outpatient mental health services ages 6 and up, call CHS Centralized Screening at 206-362-7282 Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Have your Medicaid/Apple Health or insurance number ready. You may be given an assessment appointment or open access hours depending on the location. CHS says these assessments do not meet requirements for competency or parenting plan evaluations.

name: Asian Counseling and Referral Service
address: 3639 Martin Luther King Jr Way S, Seattle, WA 98144
notes: Substance use services for adults only.

name: Center for Human Services
notes: Temporarily closed due to fire.

name: Consejo Counseling and Referral Services
notes: Primarily serves Hispanic/Latino community, all ages.

name: Consejo Counseling and Referral Services
address: 12360 Lake City Way NE, Seattle, WA 98125
notes: Contact the Lake City clinic directly at 206-461-4880 or visit consejocounseling.org to get started. Open Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Offers mental health outpatient services.

name: Consejo Counseling and Referral Services
notes: Contact the Bellevue clinic directly at 425-679-0801 or visit consejocounseling.org to get started. Open Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Offers mental health outpatient services, domestic violence survivor services, children, youth, and family services, and transitional housing.

name: Consejo Counseling and Referral Services
notes: Contact the Renton clinic directly at 206-461-4880 or visit consejocounseling.org to get started. Consejo says the next step is a no-cost initial consultation to review eligibility, understand your needs, and determine how they can best support you. Open Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM.

name: Eating Recovery Center of Washington
notes: Eating disorders only. By referral only.

name: Evergreen Treatment Services
notes: REACH serves King County residents with significant medical or mental health needs who are experiencing homelessness. Clients must be age 18 and older, or ages 16 to 18 with parental consent. Service hours are Monday, Wednesday, and Friday from 9:00 AM to 3:00 PM, and Tuesday and Thursday from 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM. Office hours are Monday through Friday, 8:30 AM to 4:30 PM.

name: Friends of Youth
address: 401 Ballarat Avenue North, North Bend, WA 98045

name: EvergreenHealth Behavioral Health
notes: Offers virtual and in-person therapy, psychiatric care, and social work support with coordination between behavioral health and primary care. Services are available only by referral from EvergreenHealth Primary Care, Canyon Park or EvergreenHealth Primary Care, Kenmore providers; outside referrals are not accepted.

name: King County Sexual Assault Resource Center
notes: Services for sexual assault and child sexual abuse survivors.

name: Muckleshoot Indian Tribe Behavioral Health
notes: Primarily serves Native American/Alaskan Native community.

name: NAVOS
notes: Substance use services for adults only.

name: Refugee Women's Alliance (ReWA)
notes: Services for refugee and immigrant populations.

name: Refugee Women's Alliance (ReWA)
notes: Services for refugee and immigrant populations.

name: Ryther
notes: New clients can call Ryther at 206-517-0234 Monday through Friday, 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM Pacific. If you prefer to start online or have trouble reaching the intake team, Ryther also offers a General Intake Form. Ryther says to allow up to 5 business days for contact.

name: SeaMar Behavioral Health
notes: Infant mental health, youth and family services.

name: Seneca Family of Agencies
notes: Youth and family services only.

name: Snoqualmie Tribe Behavioral Health
address: 9450 Ethan Wade Way SE, Snoqualmie, WA 98065
notes: Primarily serves Native American/Alaskan Native community.

name: Sound
address: 11000 Lake City Way NE, Seattle, WA 98125
notes: You can fill out Sound's online form at sound.health/get-care/client-resources, walk into a clinic during walk-in hours for a same-day screening, or call the Admissions Team at 206-901-2000. Bring what you can, such as a photo ID, insurance card, contact information for other providers, or a medication list, but these are not required to receive care. Staff may ask about current or past treatment and insurance, including Medicaid/Apple Health, Medicare, private insurance, or no insurance. Sound says no one is turned away from established services due to inability to pay, and sliding scale support may be available.

name: Valley Cities Counseling and Consultation
notes: Call first to speak with a screener at (253) 833-7444. Intake support is available Monday through Thursday, 8:30 AM to 6:30 PM, and Friday, 8:30 AM to 5:00 PM. In-person intake assessments are available at neighborhood clinics; call ahead for availability. You can ask for an interpreter or other accommodations when you call. Substance use services are for adults only.

name: Vashon Youth and Family Services
notes: Substance use services for adults only.

name: YMCA of Greater Seattle
notes: Use the YMCA behavioral health form, email counseling@seattleymca.org, or call 206-382-5340 Monday through Friday, 8:30 AM to 4:30 PM, to request an assessment for mental health counseling or substance use treatment. The YMCA serves all ages in King County, including both members and non-members. Medicaid is accepted, and sliding scale financial aid may be available. YMCA says its customer support team will usually contact families within 1 business day to answer questions and schedule an initial consultation.

name: YMCA of Greater Seattle
notes: Use the YMCA behavioral health form, email counseling@seattleymca.org, or call 206-382-5340 Monday through Friday, 8:30 AM to 4:30 PM, to request an assessment for mental health counseling or substance use treatment. The YMCA serves all ages in King County, including both members and non-members. Medicaid is accepted, and sliding scale financial aid may be available. YMCA says its customer support team will usually contact families within 1 business day to answer questions and schedule an initial consultation.

name: YMCA of Greater Seattle
notes: Use the YMCA behavioral health form, email counseling@seattleymca.org, or call 206-382-5340 Monday through Friday, 8:30 AM to 4:30 PM, to request an assessment for mental health counseling or substance use treatment. The YMCA serves all ages in King County, including both members and non-members. Medicaid is accepted, and sliding scale financial aid may be available. YMCA says its customer support team will usually contact families within 1 business day to answer questions and schedule an initial consultation. Crisis services for youth and special populations are also available.

name: Youth Eastside Services
notes: New clients and caregivers can call 425-747-4937 to schedule an initial assessment or speak with the Open Access team. Assessments are for children and youth from birth through age 22 who live in Bellevue, Kirkland, Redmond, or Sammamish, and take place at the YES Bellevue location at 999 164th Ave NE, Bellevue, WA 98008. Walk-in assessments are available Monday from 10:00 AM to 12:00 PM on a first-come, first-served basis. YES accepts most insurance plans, Medicaid, and medical coupons, and also offers a sliding scale. No family is turned away because of inability to pay.
